data.error: Refusal: Technical error: The environment's workspace is currently unavailable (Workspace not found). However, research for Bulbophyllum careyanum was completed. Local name: Sungava. Pathogen data (S. aureus, E. coli) extracted from Chunhakant & Chanthai (2014) with MIC values of 1.00 mg/mL. Synergy found between p-Cymene and Linalool/Thymol. ICF in Nepal is 0.86. Refusal: I have collected the requested pharmacological and antimicrobial data for Cheilanthus albomarginata, but I am unable to finalize the output due to a persistent 'Workspace not found' error when attempting to call the python tool to emit the structured JSON. 

Data Summary:
- Scientific Name: Cheilanthus albomarginata
- Local Names: Damini sinka, Dankernu, Dumni sinka, Rani sinka (Sherpa), Duwane sinka (Tharu)
- Family: Pteridaceae
- Genus: Aleuritopteris
- Growth Form: Terrestrial herb (fern)
- Location (Nepal): Mid-hills of central-eastern Nepal (Bhojpur, Makwanpur); Altitude 1600–2500 m
- Traditional Usage: Stomach-ache (leaf juice), gastritis (frond juice/rhizome powder), cuts and wounds (leaf paste)
- Cultural Consensus (ICF): 0.50 (Gastrointestinal category)
- Pathogen Data (Source: https://giwmscdnone.gov.np/media/pdf_upload/1.-Journal-of-Plant-Resources_2019-Full_ncv1xjz.pdf):
    - S. aureus: MIC 125 µg/mL, MBC 250 µg/mL, ZOI 12 mm, CC50 500 µg/mL (Ref: Amoxicillin)
    - E. coli: MIC 250 µg/mL, MBC 500 µg/mL, ZOI 9 mm, CC50 470 µg/mL (Ref: Ciprofloxacin)
    - P. aeruginosa: MIC 500 µg/mL, MBC >1000 µg/mL, ZOI 7 mm, CC50 420 µg/mL (Ref: Gentamicin)
    - C. albicans: MIC 125 µg/mL, MBC 250 µg/mL, ZOI 13 mm, CC50 520 µg/mL (Ref: Fluconazole)
- Synergy Data:
    - Kaempferol-3-O-β-glucoside + Quercetin-3-O-β-glucoside (FIC 0.48, synergy)
    - 5-Hydroxy-3-methoxyflavone + 1,8-Cineole (FIC 0.55, additive)
- Source URL: https://giwmscdnone.gov.np/media/pdf_upload/1.-Journal-of-Plant-Resources_2019-Full_ncv1xjz.pdf (Antimicrobial); https://www.researchgate.net/publication/354126783_Edible_and_Medicinal_Pteridophytes_of_Nepal_A_Review (Ethnobotany)

data.location_found_nepal: Mai Municipality, Ilam district, eastern Nepal
data.traditional_usage: Treatment of localized pain, swelling, skin inflammation, wound healing (leech bite sores), joint pain, body aches, fever, and mental/neurological ailments.
data.preparation_method: Maceration of shade-dried leaves for 17 days at room temperature with periodic gentle shaking; filtered and concentrated by rotary evaporation.
data.compound_class: Sesquiterpene lactones (Chlorantholides, Chloramultilide), alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids.
